channel.title: Frontion News
channel.description: Latest Headlines and Strategic Analysis of the Events Reshaping the World

item.title: French Peacekeeper Killed in Lebanon Attack During Ceasefire
item.description: A French UNIFIL peacekeeper was killed and three others wounded in a small arms attack near Ghandouriyeh in southern Lebanon. French President Macron blamed Hezbollah, which denied involvement. The attack came just after a 10-day Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire took effect.

item.title: Trump Predicts 'Special' Meeting with Xi Jinping Ahead of China Visit
item.description: President Trump said he looks forward to meeting President Xi Jinping in China next month, predicting the summit would be 'historic' and achieve much. The trip was delayed from March due to the Iran war. Analysts say the meeting could stabilize China-US relations through 2029.

item.title: China Could Gain 'Massive Strategic Advantage' from Iran War, Analyst Says
item.description: A Chinese analyst said Beijing should draw military lessons from the Iran conflict, noting that US engagement in warfare narrows the equipment gap between Chinese and American forces. With the ceasefire likely to extend, China may assume a more active role in peace talks.

item.title: Venezuela's Machado Draws Thousands in Madrid, Declines Meeting with Spain's PM
item.description: Exiled Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado rallied thousands in Madrid while PM Sánchez hosted a progressive summit in Barcelona. Machado praised Trump for removing Maduro and said she declined meeting Sánchez because of the leftist gathering.

item.title: Iran Says No Date Set for Next Round of US Nuclear Negotiations
item.description: Iran's deputy foreign minister said no date has been set for the next round of negotiations with the United States, adding that a framework of understanding must be agreed first before face-to-face talks can resume.

item.title: India Summons Iranian Envoy After Navy Fires on Indian Oil Tankers in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Two Indian-flagged vessels, including a crude oil tanker carrying 2 million barrels of Iraqi oil, were fired upon by the Iranian Navy north of Oman. New Delhi lodged a strong protest with Iran's envoy, noting Iran had previously promised not to target friendly nations' ships.

item.title: US Military Prepares to Board and Seize Iran-Linked Ships Worldwide
item.description: The Pentagon is planning to intercept and take control of commercial vessels tied to Iran, potentially far beyond the Middle East, as part of an economic pressure campaign dubbed 'Economic Fury' aimed at forcing Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and make concessions in nuclear talks.

item.title: Progressive Leaders Rally in Barcelona to Defend Multilateral Order Against Trump's 'Unilateralism'
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez hosted progressive leaders from Brazil, Mexico, South Africa and other nations in Barcelona, with attendees warning that attacks on the multilateral system and international law have become normalized. Trump attacked Spain on social media during the summit.

item.title: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again as Ships Mid-Transit Reverse Course
item.description: Iran's military declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again, with maritime tracking showing ships abandoning attempts to pass through the vital waterway. The move came after the US maintained its naval blockade of Iranian ports, casting doubt on Trump's optimism about an imminent peace deal.

item.title: Trump Says Iran 'Got a Little Cute' by Re-blocking Hormuz, Claims Talks Going 'Really Well'
item.description: President Trump downplayed Iran's reversal on the Strait of Hormuz, saying the Islamic Republic 'got a little cute' but negotiations remain on track. Egypt and Pakistan said they hope to secure a final agreement 'in the coming days' as a 10-day ceasefire nears expiration.

item.title: Turkey Warns US Withdrawal from European Security Would Be 'Destructive'
item.description: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan said discussions are underway on managing a possible US withdrawal from European security architecture, calling the potential move 'destructive' for regional stability.

item.title: Iran Reimposes Restrictions on Strait of Hormuz, Vessels Report Gunfire
item.description: Iran has reimposed 'strict control' on the Strait of Hormuz just one day after announcing its reopening, with shipping sources reporting at least two vessels came under fire as the waterway closed again.

item.title: Saudi Finance Minister: Oil-Producing Countries Need Time to Ramp Up Output
item.description: Saudi Finance Minister Mohammed Al-Jadaan welcomed Iran's reopening of the Strait of Hormuz but warned it will take time for oil-producing nations to increase output, noting the Middle East situation remains 'very fragile' until a durable de-escalation is achieved.

item.title: The Iran War Has Revealed Trump's Pressure Point: The Economy
item.description: Iran has taken a beating militarily but demonstrated it can exact economic costs that Trump and his aides underestimated, raising the political risk back home and creating a new dynamic in the conflict.

item.title: Displaced Lebanese Return Home as Ceasefire Is Tested
item.description: More displaced Lebanese are heading home as the ceasefire appears to mostly hold, though Israel says it carried out strikes on what it called 'terrorists' approaching the border area.

item.title: Sanctioned Tankers Cross Strait of Hormuz Before Shots Fired
item.description: Ship-tracking data shows at least three sanctioned vessels passed through the Strait of Hormuz before Iran reimposed restrictions and gunfire was reported at vessels in the waterway.

item.title: Iranian Official Says US 'Maximalist' Demands Stall Face-to-Face Talks
item.description: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister told the AP on the margins of a Turkey diplomacy forum that maximalist US demands are preventing face-to-face negotiations, with no date yet set for a new round of talks.

item.title: Climate Now Embedded in Geopolitics and Energy Policy, Says Former COP30 Official
item.description: Climate has moved beyond a 'niche theme' and is now embedded in geopolitics, energy, and global policy discussions, said a former COP30 official in an interview with Anadolu Agency.
